name: Dihexa
subtitle: Synaptogenic Peptide | Cognitive Enhancement

modifications: N-terminal hexanoic acid for BBB penetration

overview: Dihexa is a synthetic oligopeptide derived from angiotensin IV that potently enhances cognitive function by promoting synaptogenesis. It is 10 million times more potent than BDNF at promoting synapse formation through HGF/c-Met receptor activation.
mechanism: Binds to hepatocyte growth factor (HGF) with high affinity (Kd = 65 pM) and potentiates its activity at c-Met receptor, activating PI3K/AKT pathways and promoting new synaptic connections with extraordinary potency.
keyBenefits: 10 million times more potent than BDNF
Applications for Alzheimer's, TBI, age-related cognitive decline

items.name: Memory Enhancement
items.description: Improvements in spatial, working, and consolidation demonstrated across animal models.

items.name: Learning Acceleration
items.description: Enhanced acquisition through increased synaptic plasticity.

items.name: Cognitive Recovery
items.description: Restoration in impairment models including scopolamine-induced amnesia.

items.name: Amyloid Reduction
items.description: Reduced amyloid burden in Alzheimer's models.

items.name: Synaptic Preservation
items.description: Protection of synapses in neurodegeneration models.

items.name: Dendritic Spine Formation
items.description: 3-fold increase in dendritic spine formation demonstrated.

items.name: Angiogenesis Promotion
items.description: Promotes new blood vessel formation in brain.

deliveryMethods.overview: Oral capsules/tablets are the most convenient form. Take in the morning with or without food. No reconstitution required.
keyBenefits: Room temperature storage (2 year shelf life)

deliveryMethods.overview: Subcutaneous/intraperitoneal injection provides more consistent dosing. Requires refrigerated storage after reconstitution.

steps: Calculate water volume for desired concentration
Add bacteriostatic water slowly to lyophilized powder
Gently swirl to dissolve - do not shake

keyBenefits: Abdomen, thigh, or upper arm injection sites

deliveryMethods.overview: Topical cream formulation with limited research data. Experimental route.

interactions.notes: Enhanced cognitive benefits; monitor for overstimulation.
Synergistic effects; watch for neural overstimulation.
Different mechanisms; no negative interactions known.
Both affect neuroplasticity strongly; avoid concurrent use.
Some stack for cognition; limited safety data.
Both potently neuroactive; may cause overstimulation.

timeline.effects: Subtle cognitive shifts; possible headaches during adaptation
Peak cognitive benefits and enhanced learning
Effects may persist for days to weeks after discontinuation

common: Sleep disruption when dosed late in day
contraindications: Not FDA approved - research compound only
Theoretical cancer risk via c-Met activation
Cancer history (avoid due to c-Met pathway)

good: Uniform capsules with clear lot numbers and expiration dates
warning: Research chemical sources may lack pharmaceutical standards
bad: No analytical testing or purity reports

references.title: Evaluation of Metabolically Stabilized Angiotensin IV Analogs as Procognitive/Antidementia Agents
references.authors: McCoy AT, Benoist CC, Wright JW, Harding JW
references.journal: Journal of Pharmacology and Experimental Therapeutics
references.keyFindings: Oral dihexa (2 mg/kg) completely reversed scopolamine-induced cognitive deficits in Morris water maze by day 7; produced near 3-fold increase in hippocampal dendritic spines at picomolar concentrations.

references.title: AngIV-Analog Dihexa Rescues Cognitive Impairment and Recovers Memory in the APP/PS1 Mouse via the PI3K/AKT Signaling Pathway
references.authors: Gao Y, Zhang Y, Wang Z, et al.
references.keyFindings: Dihexa restored spatial learning in APP/PS1 Alzheimer's mice, increased neuronal cells and synaptophysin expression, decreased astrocyte/microglia activation, and reduced IL-1B and TNF-a via PI3K/AKT pathway.

references.title: The Procognitive and Synaptogenic Effects of Angiotensin IV-Derived Peptides Are Dependent on Activation of the Hepatocyte Growth Factor/c-Met System
references.authors: Benoist CC, Kawas LH, Zhu M, Bhatt D, Wright JW, Harding JW
references.journal: Journal of Pharmacology and Experimental Therapeutics
references.keyFindings: Dihexa binds HGF with high affinity (Kd = 65 pM), induces c-Met phosphorylation and hippocampal spinogenesis/synaptogenesis similar to HGF; effects blocked by c-Met inhibitor confirming HGF/c-Met dependence.

references.title: The Development of Small Molecule Angiotensin IV Analogs to Treat Alzheimer's and Parkinson's Diseases
references.keyFindings: Comprehensive review establishing dihexa as an orally active, BBB-permeable compound that facilitates compromised memory and motor systems via HGF/c-Met modulation.

latestResearch.title: Fosgonimeton Attenuates Amyloid-Beta Toxicity in Preclinical Models of Alzheimer's Disease
latestResearch.summary: Fosgonimeton (dihexa prodrug) enhanced pro-survival ERK/AKT signaling, reduced GSK3B activity, and significantly rescued cognitive function in Abeta25-35 rat model of Alzheimer's disease.

latestResearch.title: Safety, Tolerability, and Pharmacodynamics of Fosgonimeton in Healthy Volunteers and Alzheimer's Subjects: Phase I Clinical Trial
latestResearch.summary: Phase I RCT (NCT03298672): fosgonimeton showed fast-onset normalization of ERP P300 latency in Alzheimer's subjects, suggesting enhancement of synaptic function; well-tolerated across dose ranges.

latestResearch.title: Fosgonimeton Promotes Neurotrophic and Procognitive Effects in Models of Dementia
latestResearch.summary: Fosgonimeton demonstrated neurotrophic and procognitive effects in multiple dementia models; clinical trials for mild-to-moderate Alzheimer's disease are ongoing (NCT04488419, NCT04886063).

quickStats.typicalDose: 8-10mg oral or 2-5mg injectable (0.5mg/kg based on research)
quickStats.storage: Oral: room temperature (2 year shelf life); Injectable lyophilized: 2-8°C; Reconstituted: 2-8°C, use within 30 days
